units.text: **Proposed Work Report**
I inspected the local repo first.

Right now it’s intentionally tiny: [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md), [CLAUDE.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/CLAUDE.md), [type.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/type.md), and [note.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/note.md).
The working tree is clean on `main`.

The vault conventions are clear from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): Tolaria-compatible Markdown notes, first H1 as title, `type:` in frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and relationships expressed with wikilinks.
I’ll follow that exactly and keep the ontology seed intentionally small.
**What I propose to create**
At the repo root:
[README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md)
[cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md)
[relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md)
[contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md)
A small `types/` folder for the initial ontology:
`types/environment.md`
`types/system.md`
`types/infrastructure.md`
`types/capability.md`
`types/interface.md`
`types/project.md`
`types/artifact.md`
`types/person.md`
`types/idea.md`
`types/decision.md`
`types/task.md`
A small `entities/` folder for the seed entity notes:
`entities/cce.md`
`entities/pem.md`
`entities/colossus.md`
`entities/matrix.md`
`entities/element.md`
`entities/tolaria.md`
`entities/github.md`
`entities/memory.md`
`entities/cognitive-registry.md`
`entities/atlas.md`
`entities/corpus.md`
`entities/provenance.md`
`entities/browser-agents.md`
`entities/codex.md`
`entities/chatgpt.md`
`entities/kevin.md`
**Content approach**
`README.md` will explain the atlas, that this repo is an experimental public starter vault, that it is not the full private production atlas, and that the priority is structure over bulk content.
`cognitive-environment-atlas.md` will serve as the initial charter and ontology note, linking to the type notes and a few seed entities.
Each type note will be `type: Type` and include:
plain-English definition
when to use it
examples
what it should not be confused with
Each entity note will stay short and use simple frontmatter like:
```yaml
type: System
status: Seed
belongs_to: "[[CCE]]"
related_to:
"[[Atlas]]"
Relationships will stay sparse and obvious.
I’ll avoid speculative links unless they’re clearly implied by the names and your prompt.
**Assumptions I’d use unless you want changes**
`CCE` is the umbrella environment and should be typed as `Environment`.
`Atlas` is a project/artifact about modeling the environment, not the whole environment itself.
`Kevin` will be represented only as a minimal `Person` note with no personal details beyond authorship/stewardship context.
Tools and platforms like `Codex`, `ChatGPT`, `GitHub`, and `Tolaria` will be described neutrally and minimally, without operational details or secrets.
The folder structure is just for readability; the ontology meaning will live in types and relationships, per the vault rules.
**One thing I want to sanity-check before writing**
There’s one naming choice with non-obvious consequences: for Tolaria, type names often appear as literal `type:` values.
I’m planning to set entity notes to values like `type: Environment`, `type: System`, etc., and make the corresponding type documents have matching H1 titles.
That fits Tolaria cleanly.
If you want, I can proceed with this exact structure next and then give you the resulting diff summary plus the exact `git diff` output.
Created a small Tolaria-compatible atlas seed in the repo and kept it intentionally narrow.

I added [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md), the charter note [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md), the vocabulary/rules notes [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md) and [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md), 11 type notes in [`types/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/types), and 16 seed entity notes in [`entities/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities).

The main adjustments you requested are reflected: `Atlas` is modeled as an `Artifact`, `Proposal` is used as a status value for uncertain architecture (`Browser Agents`), and [kevin.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities/kevin.md) is extremely minimal and only describes stewardship/ownership.
I also preserved the vault conventions from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): first H1 title, `type:` frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and wikilink relationships.
Assumptions I made:
`CCE` is the top-level `Environment`.
`PEM` and `Matrix` are best treated as `System` seeds for now.
`Colossus` and `Element` are better modeled as `Infrastructure`.
`Tolaria`, `GitHub`, `Codex`, and `ChatGPT` are most useful in this first pass as `Interface` notes.
`Browser Agents` is not yet accepted architecture, so I kept it as a normal entity note with status `Proposal` rather than inventing a new type.
Questions for Kevin:
Do you want the next pass to add a tiny set of saved views in `views/`, or keep the vault note-only for now?
Should `Codex` and `ChatGPT` remain `Interface` notes, or do you want one or both reframed later as `System` notes?
Diff summary for the atlas seed:
31 new Markdown files
550 total inserted lines
New roots: `README.md`, `cognitive-environment-atlas.md`, `relationship-vocabulary.md`, `contribution-rules.md`
New folders: `types/`, `entities/`
Git/worktree notes:
I did not touch unrelated untracked items already present in the repo: `GEMINI.md`, `cases/`, and `pem.initialized`.
I did not stage or commit anything.
If you want the exact patch next, I can generate and paste the full `git diff --no-index` style output for just these newly created atlas files.
Yes.

I came up with a seed-ontology graphic that matches the atlas as it exists now: CCE at the center, grouped rings for systems, infrastructure, interfaces, capabilities, artifacts, and a minimal steward node for Kevin.
`Atlas` is emphasized as the key artifact, and `Browser Agents` is visually marked as a proposal.
If you want, I can do either of these next:
Generate a second version that feels more like a map and less like a systems diagram.
Create a cleaner export tailored for the repo README, with wider layout and fewer labels.
Here’s the second version, reworked as an isometric map-city of the atlas.
It treats `CCE` as the enclosing environment, makes `Atlas` the central civic landmark, turns the ontology into districts, and keeps `Browser Agents` visibly tentative as a proposal site.
If you want, I can next make one of these refinements:
A cleaner poster version with fewer labels and stronger visual hierarchy.
A repo-friendly diagram version sized for `README.md` headers and previews.
A third variant that feels more like a fantasy atlas plate and less like an urban planning map.
Here’s the modernized isometric city version.
It keeps the atlas-as-place idea, but uses contemporary urban symbology and architecture so it reads more like a present-day civic district map.
If you want, I can take it one step further in one of these directions:
More minimalist and Apple-like: fewer labels, cleaner blocks, stronger white space.
More urban-planning board: zoning colors, street names, sharper legend, less illustrative texture.
More polished for publication: same composition, but tuned as a repo hero image or presentation slide.
I made both variants.
The first is the minimalist Apple-like version: fewer labels, cleaner massing, stronger white space, and a calmer product-design feel.
The second is the urban-planning board version: zoning colors, street naming, sharper legends, and more explicit district structure.
If you want, I can now help you choose one as the “official” visual direction and then do a final polished pass for one specific use:
`README` hero image
slide/presentation cover
poster-style atlas plate
Tolaria vault splash image
This version fixes the self-reference problem nicely.
The atlas is now the view itself, with the center handled as a civic commons instead of an in-world `Atlas` building, and the districts have more visible color separation.
Two things I think are working especially well now:
The central open square gives the map a real civic center without implying “the map is inside the map.”
The stronger zoning contrast makes the ontology easier to scan without losing the modern planning-board feel.
If you want, I can do one more refinement pass focused on either:
Making it even more minimal and polished.
Making the street names and district labels semantically sharper to match your actual ontology language.
Producing a final export optimized for the repo `README`.
This revision is much closer.
The zones now separate more cleanly, the center reads as shared space instead of a named object, and the legend is more abstract and map-appropriate.
One thing I still notice, in case you want another pass: the left legend text still names relationship types fairly explicitly.

That may be fine, but if you want it even more diagram-neutral, I can reduce it further to visual classes like `primary link`, `support link`, `adjacent link`, `feed link`, and `reference link` without the ontology terms in parentheses.
If you want, I can do one final cleanup pass with:
Even stronger district contrast.
A more minimal relationship legend.
Slightly less visual weight on the left-side info panels so the map itself dominates.
This version shifts back toward the software-architecture feel while keeping the isometric depth.
It treats `CCE` as the core platform, groups the ontology into modular planes, and avoids the “atlas inside the atlas” issue by making the diagram itself the atlas view.
A few things are working especially well here:
The grouped isometric platforms preserve the ontology cleanly without turning into a city.
`Browser Agents` reads clearly as tentative because it sits as a lighter proposal module.
`Kevin` stays peripheral and minimal, which matches the public-lab framing.
If you want, I can refine this exact direction with one more pass:
More minimal and abstract, with less text inside each block.
More technically precise, with cleaner relationship semantics and less decorative labeling.
Dark-background presentation version for slides.

**What I propose to create**

A small `entities/` folder for the seed entity notes:

**Content approach**

- `README.md` will explain the atlas, that this repo is an experimental public starter vault, that it is not the full private production atlas, and that the priority is structure over bulk content.
- `cognitive-environment-atlas.md` will serve as the initial charter and ontology note, linking to the type notes and a few seed entities.
- Each type note will be `type: Type` and include:
  - plain-English definition

  - examples

```yaml
---
type: System
status: Seed
belongs_to: "[[CCE]]"
related_to:
  - "[[Atlas]]"
---
```
- Relationships will stay sparse and obvious. I’ll avoid speculative links unless they’re clearly implied by the names and your prompt.

**Assumptions I’d use unless you want changes**

- `CCE` is the umbrella environment and should be typed as `Environment`.
- `Atlas` is a project/artifact about modeling the environment, not the whole environment itself.
- `Kevin` will be represented only as a minimal `Person` note with no personal details beyond authorship/stewardship context.
- Tools and platforms like `Codex`, `ChatGPT`, `GitHub`, and `Tolaria` will be described neutrally and minimally, without operational details or secrets.

turns.answer:
Created a small Tolaria-compatible atlas seed in the repo and kept it intentionally narrow. I added [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md), the charter note [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md), the vocabulary/rules notes [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md) and [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md), 11 type notes in [`types/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/types), and 16 seed entity notes in [`entities/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities).

The main adjustments you requested are reflected: `Atlas` is modeled as an `Artifact`, `Proposal` is used as a status value for uncertain architecture (`Browser Agents`), and [kevin.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities/kevin.md) is extremely minimal and only describes stewardship/ownership. I also preserved the vault conventions from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): first H1 title, `type:` frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and wikilink relationships.

Assumptions I made:
- `CCE` is the top-level `Environment`.
- `PEM` and `Matrix` are best treated as `System` seeds for now.
- `Colossus` and `Element` are better modeled as `Infrastructure`.
- `Tolaria`, `GitHub`, `Codex`, and `ChatGPT` are most useful in this first pass as `Interface` notes.
- `Browser Agents` is not yet accepted architecture, so I kept it as a normal entity note with status `Proposal` rather than inventing a new type.

Questions for Kevin:
- Do you want the next pass to add a tiny set of saved views in `views/`, or keep the vault note-only for now?
- Should `Codex` and `ChatGPT` remain `Interface` notes, or do you want one or both reframed later as `System` notes?

**What I propose to create**

At the repo root:
- [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md)
- [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md)
- [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md)
- [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md)

A small `types/` folder for the initial ontology:
- `types/environment.md`
- `types/system.md`
- `types/infrastructure.md`
- `types/capability.md`
- `types/interface.md`
- `types/project.md`
- `types/artifact.md`
- `types/person.md`
- `types/idea.md`
- `types/decision.md`
- `types/task.md`

A small `entities/` folder for the seed entity notes:
- `entities/cce.md`
- `entities/pem.md`
- `entities/colossus.md`
- `entities/matrix.md`
- `entities/element.md`
- `entities/tolaria.md`
- `entities/github.md`
- `entities/memory.md`
- `entities/cognitive-registry.md`
- `entities/atlas.md`
- `entities/corpus.md`
- `entities/provenance.md`
- `entities/browser-agents.md`
- `entities/codex.md`
- `entities/chatgpt.md`
- `entities/kevin.md`

**Content approach**

- `README.md` will explain the atlas, that this repo is an experimental public starter vault, that it is not the full private production atlas, and that the priority is structure over bulk content.
- `cognitive-environment-atlas.md` will serve as the initial charter and ontology note, linking to the type notes and a few seed entities.
- Each type note will be `type: Type` and include:
  - plain-English definition
  - when to use it
  - examples
  - what it should not be confused with
- Each entity note will stay short and use simple frontmatter like:
```yaml
---
type: System
status: Seed
belongs_to: "[[CCE]]"
related_to:
  - "[[Atlas]]"
---
```
- Relationships will stay sparse and obvious. I’ll avoid speculative links unless they’re clearly implied by the names and your prompt.

**Assumptions I’d use unless you want changes**

- `CCE` is the umbrella environment and should be typed as `Environment`.
- `Atlas` is a project/artifact about modeling the environment, not the whole environment itself.
- `Kevin` will be represented only as a minimal `Person` note with no personal details beyond authorship/stewardship context.
- Tools and platforms like `Codex`, `ChatGPT`, `GitHub`, and `Tolaria` will be described neutrally and minimally, without operational details or secrets.
- The folder structure is just for readability; the ontology meaning will live in types and relationships, per the vault rules.

**One thing I want to sanity-check before writing**

There’s one naming choice with non-obvious consequences: for Tolaria, type names often appear as literal `type:` values. I’m planning to set entity notes to values like `type: Environment`, `type: System`, etc., and make the corresponding type documents have matching H1 titles. That fits Tolaria cleanly.

If you want, I can proceed with this exact structure next and then give you the resulting diff summary plus the exact `git diff` output.

history.outcome:
Created a small Tolaria-compatible atlas seed in the repo and kept it intentionally narrow. I added [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md), the charter note [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md), the vocabulary/rules notes [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md) and [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md), 11 type notes in [`types/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/types), and 16 seed entity notes in [`entities/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities).

The main adjustments you requested are reflected: `Atlas` is modeled as an `Artifact`, `Proposal` is used as a status value for uncertain architecture (`Browser Agents`), and [kevin.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities/kevin.md) is extremely minimal and only describes stewardship/ownership. I also preserved the vault conventions from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): first H1 title, `type:` frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and wikilink relationships.

Assumptions I made:
- `CCE` is the top-level `Environment`.
- `PEM` and `Matrix` are best treated as `System` seeds for now.
- `Colossus` and `Element` are better modeled as `Infrastructure`.
- `Tolaria`, `GitHub`, `Codex`, and `ChatGPT` are most useful in this first pass as `Interface` notes.
- `Browser Agents` is not yet accepted architecture, so I kept it as a normal entity note with status `Proposal` rather than inventing a new type.

Questions for Kevin:
- Do you want the next pass to add a tiny set of saved views in `views/`, or keep the vault note-only for now?
- Should `Codex` and `ChatGPT` remain `Interface` notes, or do you want one or both reframed later as `System` notes?

history.outcome:
Yes. history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
Here’s the second version, reworked as an isometric map-city of the atlas. It treats `CCE` as the enclosing environment, makes `Atlas` the central civic landmark, turns the ontology into districts, and keeps `Browser Agents` visibly tentative as a proposal site.

If you want, I can next make one of these refinements:
1. A cleaner poster version with fewer labels and stronger visual hierarchy.
2. A repo-friendly diagram version sized for `README.md` headers and previews.
3. A third variant that feels more like a fantasy atlas plate and less like an urban planning map.
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
Here’s the modernized isometric city version. It keeps the atlas-as-place idea, but uses contemporary urban symbology and architecture so it reads more like a present-day civic district map.

If you want, I can take it one step further in one of these directions:
1. More minimalist and Apple-like: fewer labels, cleaner blocks, stronger white space.
2. More urban-planning board: zoning colors, street names, sharper legend, less illustrative texture.
3. More polished for publication: same composition, but tuned as a repo hero image or presentation slide.
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
I made both variants.

The first is the minimalist Apple-like version: fewer labels, cleaner massing, stronger white space, and a calmer product-design feel. The second is the urban-planning board version: zoning colors, street naming, sharper legends, and more explicit district structure.

If you want, I can now help you choose one as the “official” visual direction and then do a final polished pass for one specific use:
- `README` hero image
- slide/presentation cover
- poster-style atlas plate
- Tolaria vault splash image
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
This version fixes the self-reference problem nicely. The atlas is now the view itself, with the center handled as a civic commons instead of an in-world `Atlas` building, and the districts have more visible color separation.

Two things I think are working especially well now:
- The central open square gives the map a real civic center without implying “the map is inside the map.”
- The stronger zoning contrast makes the ontology easier to scan without losing the modern planning-board feel.

If you want, I can do one more refinement pass focused on either:
1. Making it even more minimal and polished.
2. Making the street names and district labels semantically sharper to match your actual ontology language.
3. Producing a final export optimized for the repo `README`.
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
This revision is much closer. The zones now separate more cleanly, the center reads as shared space instead of a named object, and the legend is more abstract and map-appropriate.

One thing I still notice, in case you want another pass: the left legend text still names relationship types fairly explicitly. That may be fine, but if you want it even more diagram-neutral, I can reduce it further to visual classes like `primary link`, `support link`, `adjacent link`, `feed link`, and `reference link` without the ontology terms in parentheses.

If you want, I can do one final cleanup pass with:
1. Even stronger district contrast.
2. A more minimal relationship legend.
3. Slightly less visual weight on the left-side info panels so the map itself dominates.
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
This version shifts back toward the software-architecture feel while keeping the isometric depth. It treats `CCE` as the core platform, groups the ontology into modular planes, and avoids the “atlas inside the atlas” issue by making the diagram itself the atlas view.

A few things are working especially well here:
- The grouped isometric platforms preserve the ontology cleanly without turning into a city.
- `Browser Agents` reads clearly as tentative because it sits as a lighter proposal module.
- `Kevin` stays peripheral and minimal, which matches the public-lab framing.

If you want, I can refine this exact direction with one more pass:
1. More minimal and abstract, with less text inside each block.
2. More technically precise, with cleaner relationship semantics and less decorative labeling.
3. Dark-background presentation version for slides.
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.
